Keywords: Putting all UI logic state management and business logic in one widget or view controller
What People Do Wrong: Impossible to test; hard to maintain; merge conflicts in teams; violates SRP
Why Its Bad: Split into smaller widgets; extract business logic to ViewModel/BLoC; use composition
Severity: File > 500 lines; build method > 100 lines; > 10 state variables

Keywords: Using setState for all state management even in complex apps
What People Do Wrong: Entire widget tree rebuilds on every change; no separation of concerns; untestable logic
Why Its Bad: Use proper state management (BLoC/Riverpod/Provider); setState only for local ephemeral state
Severity: grep for setState; count occurrences per file; > 5 setState in one widget

Keywords: Not canceling stream subscriptions or disposing controllers when widget is removed
What People Do Wrong: Memory grows over time; callbacks fire on disposed widgets; app becomes sluggish and crashes
Why Its Bad: Always cancel subscriptions in dispose; use autoDispose; use CancelToken for API calls
Severity: Flutter DevTools memory tab; watch heap growth; look for dispose() in StatefulWidgets

Keywords: Forcing one platform's design on both iOS and Android
What People Do Wrong: iOS users get confused by Material behaviors; Android users miss platform features; poor UX
Why Its Bad: Use adaptive widgets; follow platform conventions; test on both platforms
Severity: Test on both platforms; check for platform-specific widgets

Keywords: Hardcoding user-facing strings instead of using localization
What People Do Wrong: Cannot translate app; inconsistent text; typos spread across codebase
Why Its Bad: Use intl/easy_localization; centralize all strings; use string keys
Severity: grep for quoted strings in widget files; check for AppLocalizations usage

Keywords: Catching and ignoring errors or not handling them at all
What People Do Wrong: Bugs go unnoticed; users see blank screens; crashes in production without reports
Why Its Bad: Handle every error explicitly; show user-friendly messages; report to Crashlytics/Sentry
Severity: grep for empty catch blocks; grep for 'print(e)' in catch

Keywords: Adding too many layers and abstractions for simple features
What People Do Wrong: Development is 3x slower; team struggles to understand; simple CRUD takes days
Why Its Bad: Match architecture complexity to project size; start simple and refactor when needed
Better Approach:
// For a TODO app:
lib/
  features/todo/
    data/datasources/todo_remote_datasource.dart
    data/datasources/todo_local_datasource.dart
    data/models/todo_model.dart
    data/repositories/todo_repository_impl.dart
    domain/entities/todo_entity.dart
    domain/repositories/todo_repository.dart
    domain/usecases/get_todos_usecase.dart
    domain/usecases/add_todo_usecase.dart
    presentation/bloc/todo_bloc.dart
    presentation/bloc/todo_event.dart
    presentation/bloc/todo_state.dart
    // 15+ files for a simple TODO
Code Bad:
// Simple TODO app - just enough structure
lib/
  models/todo.dart
  providers/todo_provider.dart
  screens/todo_screen.dart
  widgets/todo_tile.dart
  // 4 files, perfectly maintainable
Severity: Count files per feature; if > 10 files for simple CRUD, probably over-engineered

Keywords: Using BuildContext after an async gap when the widget may be unmounted
What People Do Wrong: Crashes with 'looking up deactivated widget'; unpredictable navigation,Check mounted before using context; use ref (Riverpod) or BLoC for async side effects"
Code Good: Flutter analyzer warnings; runtime exceptions about deactivated widgets

Keywords: Writing boilerplate code manually instead of using code generation
What People Do Wrong: Tedious manual work; inconsistent implementations; bugs in serialization
Why Its Bad: Use freezed for models; json_serializable for JSON; injectable for DI; build_runner
Severity: Count boilerplate in model classes; check if freezed/json_serializable is in pubspec

Keywords: Deeply nested widget trees that are impossible to read and maintain
What People Do Wrong: Unreadable code; hard to modify; impossible to review; performance issues from rebuilds
Why Its Bad: Extract widgets into named classes; use Widget methods; limit nesting to 5-7 levels

Keywords: Using API response models directly in UI widgets coupling UI to API
What People Do Wrong: API changes break UI; cannot reshape data for UI needs; testing requires API mocking
Why Its Bad: Map API models to domain entities; use separate view models for UI
Severity: Check if API model classes are imported in presentation/ layer

Keywords: Running heavy computation on the main/UI thread causing jank and ANR
What People Do Wrong: App freezes; dropped frames; ANR dialogs on Android; poor user experience
Why Its Bad: Use compute()/isolates for heavy work; run I/O on background threads
Severity: DevTools performance overlay; jank during data loading; ANR reports

Keywords: App completely breaks when there's no internet connection
What People Do Wrong: Users in poor connectivity areas can't use the app; data loss; frustration
Why Its Bad: Cache responses; implement offline queue; show cached data with stale indicator
Severity: Test with airplane mode; check for connectivity_plus usage

Keywords: Adding too many packages without evaluation leading to bloated apps and conflicts
What People Do Wrong: Large app size; version conflicts; maintenance burden; security risks from unmaintained packages
Why Its Bad: Evaluate packages before adding; prefer well-maintained packages; audit regularly
Better Approach:
# pubspec.yaml with 80+ dependencies
dependencies:
  flutter_awesome_button: ^1.0.0  # 2 stars, last updated 2021
  super_utils: ^0.1.0  # Unmaintained
  my_custom_logger: ^1.0.0  # Could use built-in debugPrint
  another_http_client: ^2.0.0  # Already have dio
Code Bad:
# Curated dependencies
dependencies:
  flutter_bloc: ^8.0.0  # Well-maintained, 10k+ likes
  dio: ^5.0.0  # Standard HTTP client
  get_it: ^7.0.0  # Popular DI
  # Evaluate: pub.dev score, maintenance, GitHub stars
Severity: Count dependencies in pubspec.yaml; check pub.dev scores; look for unmaintained packages

Keywords: Showing inconsistent or technical error messages to users
What People Do Wrong: Users confused; poor UX; exposes technical details
Why Its Bad: Centralize error messages; use user-friendly language; localize errors
Severity: Search for e.toString() or e.message in UI code
